title: Tort (Non-Personal Injury)

statuteRef.label: Time limit for actions founded on tort

questions.label: Date cause of action accrued
questions.helpText: The date on which the damage occurred or the cause of action otherwise accrued. In tort, this is typically when damage is first sustained, not when the wrongful act occurred (if different).

questions.label: Could the damage be latent (not immediately apparent)?
questions.helpText: Latent damage is damage that is not discoverable at the time it occurs. If latent damage may apply, the Latent Damage Act 1986 and s.14A Limitation Act 1980 may provide an alternative knowledge-based limitation period. This tool does not calculate that regime in v1.

questions.label: Was the claimant under a disability at the date of accrual?
questions.helpText: Under the Limitation Act 1980, a person is under a disability if they are a minor (under 18) or lack capacity within the meaning of the Mental Capacity Act 2005.

questions.label: Date disability ceased
questions.helpText: The date the claimant turned 18, or the date capacity was regained.

questions.label: Was there fraud, deliberate concealment, or mistake?
questions.helpText: Under s.32 Limitation Act 1980, if the action is based on fraud, or the defendant deliberately concealed relevant facts, or the action is for relief from the consequences of a mistake, the limitation period does not begin until the claimant discovered or could with reasonable diligence have discovered the fraud, concealment, or mistake.

questions.label: Date of discovery (or when facts could reasonably have been discovered)
questions.helpText: The date the claimant discovered, or could with reasonable diligence have discovered, the fraud, concealment, or mistake.

notes: This rule covers the standard 6-year limitation period for tort claims that do not involve personal injury.
If latent damage may apply, the special knowledge-based regime under s.14A may provide a different limitation period. This is not calculated in v1.
Where tort and contract claims arise from the same facts, they may have different accrual dates and different limitation periods.
